## [00:00 - 03:04]

Okay, so the first step to launching your rapid fire round number one where you're testing out the personas for your client is to open up the ad tracking sheet that you guys have a template for and on the second tab what you'll see is the tab called rapid fire testing. So the first tab is our funnel outline. Now this is going to get done once we have a clear understanding of the personas we're going to go after once we activate the funnel. For now we're interested in this tab and then the last tab is your tracking. So this is to keep a track of things as you go. So what we want to do here is we basically really want to figure out how much budget we're going to allocate and how many ad sets we can actually split test. So we know that we have a total budget of $200 that is allocated to this round which means we don't have a lot of budget to split test too many ad sets. Now this is a hair salon so it is restricted to a geographical area which means that's not necessarily a bad thing because honestly you can't really split test too many things whereas if I if I was working with a client that was nationwide I could split test a lot more like local acts I could split test against an interest-based audience I could split test against blanket marketing whereas with this there's just not much room like the audiences are so small that you pretty much have to do blanket marketing for most of them except we can we can get away with we'll do demographic here that's not interest you can get away with some demographical targeting so we've got one two three four five ad sets that we're going to be split testing and this is the budget I'm allocating to each one of these this one should be $15 now when you start to build these you might find that some of these are not going to work because they're too small that's okay just go in this sheet edit this sheet and and it will tell you how much budget you have so basically we're spending $75 a day as long as this feeds out properly to the audience which means that we'll have a rapid fire test for about two and a half days right because our total budget is $200 now you can set these up as lifetime budgets as well my only thing with that is that I find that lifetime budgets don't feed out very well because it really taps into the pacing part of the algorithm so I prefer daily budgets and then just creating a task internally to remind myself to go in and turn off the rapid fire testing okay if you go over budget by five ten dollars that's fine you can just pull that budget from your next round right so this is essentially a way to kind of outline what you're going to be building inside now if we're inside the ad account you'll see here I've set up the campaign so I've given it the naming conventions that I was using when I built out that lucid chart right so TOF

## [06:04 - 07:38]

the ad creative because this is not so let's delete this edit this okay once I've created the ad I'm just gonna publish this I'm gonna go inside of the ad tracking sheet that we set up for our client and I'm gonna punch in the ad I'm gonna send this off to my team once this is all done to double check if I don't have anyone on my team to double check the grammar the spelling on all this creative I will essentially just give it a day and or an afternoon come back to it when I'm a bit more refreshed and then go through and double check for that and then I'll send this off to the client for approval now just make sure that you put everything on pause before the client approves okay so that's really important guys make sure your ads are not live feeding out budget until your client has approved the creative so this is essentially how to set up the first rapid fire round we're using a messenger objective campaign you're gonna go and build out the skeleton inside of the sheet allocate a budget to it and then create all your personas you're targeting around that and then the ad creatives and then add those ad creatives to the tracking sheet so that you can then send this off to your team for approval and then the client and then you're ready to go live once all of that is done
